body.title: agent_datacenter: Igor block is manual-only — no auto-unblock
body.result: IgorDevice.block() ships blocked.flag; shim has no auto-block — satisfies manual-only constraint
body.description:
Igor's DeviceConfig sets manual_block_only=True. Auto-block can still trigger (restart loop protection fires), but unblock always requires manual action. Igor is intentionally blocked for DB migrations — auto-unblock would be dangerous.

Proposed shape:
- In IgorDevice's DeviceConfig: DeviceConfig(manual_block_only=True)
- In RestartGuard: when device config has manual_block_only=True, auto_block() still fires on loop detection, but unblock() requires an explicit manual call (agentctl unblock igor-wild-0001 or rack.unblock MCP tool)
- rack.health() on Igor shows block_type='auto' with note 'manual unblock required'
- This is enforced in Skeleton.unblock_device(): check config.manual_block_only; if True, only accept unblock from rack.* caller

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/igor/device.py (DeviceConfig instantiation)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/skeleton.py (unblock_device check)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: comment explaining why Igor is manual_block_only (DB migrations need controlled restart)

Scope boundary:
IN: manual_block_only enforcement in unblock_device(); DeviceConfig flag
OUT: the auto-block trigger itself (T-adc-restart-loop-protection covers that)

Test plan:
- IgorDevice DeviceConfig has manual_block_only=True
- Auto-block fires on Igor → block_type='auto' in rack.health()
- Skeleton.unblock_device('igor') from non-rack caller → rejected (AuthError)
- Skeleton.unblock_device('igor') from skeleton itself → succeeds
